steps.title: Identify the right triangle setup
steps.content: Triangle JKL has a right angle at K (shown by the square). The hypotenuse is $JL = 24$. Angle $y$ is located at vertex $L$, and we need to find side $\overline{KL}$.

steps.title: Apply the cosine ratio definition
steps.content:
In a right triangle, cosine of an angle equals the ratio of the adjacent side to the hypotenuse. For angle $y$ at vertex $L$:
$$\cos y = \frac{\text{adjacent}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{KL}{JL}$$

steps.title: Substitute and solve for KL
steps.content: We know $\cos y^\circ = \frac{23}{24}$ and $JL = 24$. Substituting into the cosine ratio: $$\frac{KL}{24} = \frac{23}{24}$$ Multiplying both sides by 24: $$KL = 23$$ That matches choice 23 — the length of $\overline{KL}$ is 23.
